commit.message:
refactor(acp): land canonical operation model core (#95) ## Summary Land the core of the god-clean canonical operation model so ACP tool semantics now flow through one typed runtime boundary instead of parallel provider-specific transcript paths and duplicated UI derivations. This also closes the original rendering bug behind this branch: descriptive tool calls no longer get promoted into subagent-task UI just because their payload happened to contain generic task-like fields. ## Architecture ```mermaid flowchart TB Providers[Provider payloads / history replay] Parser[Parser boundary
provider evidence + typed semantics] Updates[Canonical SessionUpdate stream] Store[OperationStore
identity + lifecycle reducer] Projection[ToolCallManager / presentation builder] UI[Desktop scenes and tool UI] Providers --> Parser --> Updates --> Store --> Projection --> UI ``` ## What changed 1. Introduced the canonical operation contract and edit model across Rust and generated desktop types, including provider evidence capture, first-class `EditDelta`, explicit operation identity, and typed operation state owned by `OperationStore`. 2. Moved replay-heavy providers onto the same semantic path as live updates by converting Codex and OpenCode history into canonical `SessionUpdate` events and replaying them through the shared accumulator instead of hand-building transcript tool-call state. 3. Centralized read-side tool presentation in `tool-presentation.ts`, then wired the registry, activity projection, agent panel scene, and desktop renderer routing through that single builder so task vs non-task display decisions come from one place. 4. Added fixture-backed provider conformance coverage for Copilot, Codex, and OpenCode plus targeted TS regressions that lock task/non-task routing, edit projection behavior, and legacy compatibility for older stored edit payloads. 5. Kept the direct-replacement architecture rule and plan in sync with the shipped implementation so the branch describes one canonical runtime, not a coexistence path. ## Reviewer focus - `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/session_update/tool_calls.rs` and `packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/store/operation-store.svelte.ts` now define the canonical operation lifecycle and edit semantics. - `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/codex_history/parser.rs` and `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/session_converter/opencode.rs` are the biggest replay-path replacements. - `packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/components/tool-calls/tool-presentation.ts` is now the authoritative UI presentation seam. ## Verification - `cd packages/desktop/src-tauri && cargo test codex_history::parser --quiet` - `cd packages/desktop/src-tauri && cargo test opencode_history::parser --quiet` - `cd packages/desktop/src-tauri && cargo test session_converter:: --quiet` - `cd packages/desktop/src-tauri && cargo test --test provider_operation_conformance --quiet` - `cd packages/desktop && bun test src/lib/acp/components/modified-files/logic/__tests__/aggregate-file-edits.test.ts src/lib/acp/components/tool-calls/__tests__/permission-display.test.ts src/lib/acp/components/session-list/__tests__/session-list-logic.test.ts src/lib/acp/components/tool-calls/__tests__/build-agent-tool-entry.test.ts` - `cd packages/desktop && bun run check` --- [![Compound Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin) 🤖 Generated with GPT-5.4 via [GitHub Copilot CLI](https://github.com/features/copilot) --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Acepe Test Co-authored-by: Test

commit.message:
fix: restore NPM_TOKEN auth and add --access public for scoped package publish (#7475)

* fix: explicitly dispatch CI after tag push to build release binaries

GITHUB_TOKEN pushes do not trigger new workflow runs (GitHub prevents
recursive loop triggers). This means the tag push from the release
workflow never triggered osrm-backend.yml, so no binaries were built
or uploaded to the release assets.

Changes:
- Add workflow_dispatch trigger to osrm-backend.yml
- Add 'actions: write' permission to release workflow
- After pushing the tag, explicitly dispatch osrm-backend.yml on the
  tag ref; CI runs with GITHUB_REF=refs/tags/v* so PUBLISH=On and
  prebuilt binaries are built and uploaded
- Restore NODE_AUTH_TOKEN and add --access public for scoped npm package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove redundant NPM_TOKEN and --access public from publish step

publishConfig.access=public is already set in package.json, so --access
public is redundant. OIDC via setup-node handles authentication when
id-token: write is set.

commit.message:
fix: stabilize flaky Fluent UI Dialog portal tests

Replace userEvent.click() with fireEvent.click() for dialog-trigger
clicks in agentConfigCard, templateCard, and settingsPanel tests.

Root cause: userEvent simulates a full pointer/mouse/focus event
sequence that doesn't reliably trigger React state updates for
dialog-opening clicks under parallel test load in jsdom. The
multi-event simulation creates timing windows where the Portal
mount can be delayed past the findByRole timeout.

fireEvent.click() dispatches a single synchronous click event,
which deterministically triggers the onClick handler and React
state update. Dialog content interactions (clicking buttons within

commit.message:
fix: don't push subminor version tag on scheduled builds (#64)

During scheduled (cron) runs github.ref_type=='branch' and
github.ref_name=='main' are both true, so the previous condition
  (github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.ref_name == 'main') || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
incorrectly enabled the exact version tag (e.g. 2.0.8) on every
nightly build, re-pushing an immutable tag that should only be
created once.

Fix: key the enable flag on the event name instead.
- workflow_run  → new commit landed on main, push all tags including subminor
- workflow_dispatch → manual run, keep subminor for convenience
- schedule → nightly security-rebuild, push only :latest :major :major.minor and SHA

fix(logging): add OTEL disable env var overrides (issue #400) (#401)

* fix(logging): add OTEL disable env var overrides (issue #400)

Add OTEL_SDK_DISABLED, OTEL_LOGS_DISABLED, OTEL_TRACES_DISABLED, and
OTEL_METRICS_DISABLED env var overrides to applyDefaults in
libs/go/logging. These take priority over Config field values, allowing
operators to disable OTEL signals at runtime without code changes.

OTEL_SDK_DISABLED follows the OpenTelemetry spec and disables all three
signals. When the OTLP collector is unavailable (e.g. bare-metal hosts),
setting OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true prevents the exporter from dialling
127.0.0.1:4317, eliminating the log buffer saturation and cascading
RabbitMQ heartbeat loss described in the bug.

Closes #400
